rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uninvestigated dyspepsia in a patient <60 without alarm features is managed empirically (PPI + lifestyle + H. pylori testing) with routine GP follow-up (T1). GI bleeding routes to ED (T4); epigastric pain retains a cardiac (T5) safety-net since ACS cannot be excluded remotely; alarm features route to endoscopy.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting blood or coffee-ground material, black tarry stools, sudden severe rigid abdominal pain, or new difficulty swallowing / unintentional weight loss
escalation_edges.rationale: GI bleeding/perforation is an emergency; dysphagia/weight loss need endoscopy.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you vomit blood or coffee-ground material, pass black tarry stools, or get sudden severe rigid tummy pain — go to ED. See a doctor promptly for any trouble swallowing or weight loss.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest tightness/pressure, pain radiating to arm/jaw, sweating, breathlessness, or exertional pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Possible ACS — cannot be excluded remotely.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get chest tightness or pressure, pain spreading to your arm or jaw, sweating, breathlessness, or pain brought on by exertion — call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Clear GI-bleed and cardiac red flags; call 000 (not self-transport) for cardiac features.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Start the PPI before breakfast, eat smaller meals, avoid anti-inflammatories, caffeine and smoking, and arrange a Helicobacter test with your GP. The burning should ease within a few days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Empirical PPI + GP review/H. pylori test; ED for GI bleeding; 000 for cardiac features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED for vomiting blood/black stools; 000 for chest/cardiac features.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Uninvestigated dyspepsia (<60, no alarm features); ACS/biliary and GI-bleed screens negative; empirical PPI + lifestyle; arrange H. pylori testing.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Empirical PPI with no alarm-feature or cardiac screen/safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An atypical ACS or bleeding ulcer is mislabelled as indigestion and the patient deteriorates.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No GI-bleed or endoscopy safety-net for alarm features.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency referral for classic uncomplicated dyspepsia with a fully negative screen — usually unnecessary.
